description: Successful publish response MUST contain exactly the five registry-assigned fields (ctx_id, lineage_id, version, created_at, status) and MUST NOT echo content_hash, signature, or any body field. status MUST be 'active' on a fresh publication. The response schema is additionalProperties: false; consumer deserializers MUST NOT rely on additional fields appearing.

input.body_summary: A schema-valid first-version publish request with a verifiable signature. Concrete payload omitted; any pub-001-style well-formed body whose signature verifies is acceptable as input to this fixture.

headers.Location: MUST be the percent-encoded canonical retrieval URL for the assigned ctx_id (e.g. /contexts/acdp%3A%2F%2F<authority>%2F<uuid>)

field_constraints.ctx_id: Form: acdp://<authority>/<uuid>; <authority> equals the registry's declared authority.
field_constraints.lineage_id: Form: lin:sha256:<64-lowercase-hex>; deterministically derived per RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.6.
field_constraints.version: Integer == 1 for first-version publication; previous.version + 1 otherwise.
field_constraints.status: MUST be the literal string 'active' on a successful publish response.

rationale: Echoing content_hash from a publish response confers no integrity guarantee — the producer already submitted it and signed it. Reference implementations have included content_hash in publish-response structs, masking shape divergences via test mocks that mirror the same wrong shape. This fixture pins the exact response surface.
